review_text: It's an interesting film that could easily be based on real events, but it's a captivating fiction due to the themes and concerns it raises. Its technical merits stand out, as does Cate Blanchett's performance. [Full Review in Spanish]

Blanchett rules the screen and Field’s eye for the bodacious, striking visual means this Icarus journey is a hell of a ride, in spite of its cynicism and contempt for contemporary culture.
Field hasn’t made a movie in over sixteen years, and his return to the big screen is not only welcome, but a grandiose piece of cinema from a real maestro who has been gone for far too long.

This film is so brilliant because every aspect of it is so perfect, so well crafted and honed into that perfection. What an absolute marvel this film is.
This script is so incredibly well written and fascinating. I loved the ride this film takes you on as you witness a person become completely unraveled and already can’t wait for the second viewing.

This Oscar-worthy performance by Blanchett gets its power from her complete embrace of the character. She goes so deep into the character, the film begins to feel more like fact than fiction.
Todd Field uses smart scriptwriting and the extraordinary acting skills of Cate Blanchett to conduct a virtual symphony of cinema that resonates long after the closing credits.

TAR is an actor's movie that should be celebrated. It is beautifully put together and performed and, despite its length, is wholly worth a watch.

Tár does not make heavy-handed accusations about the state of our society and people in power. Instead, it asks the audience to look at Lydia, to see her as the totality of who she is.
